meta.note: Brazil + Chile + Cuba sweep, published books. Brazil = military-dictatorship (DCDP) bans from Reimão's authoritative SciELO study (undated DCDP-era titles use 1970 as a proxy, noted). Chile = Pinochet junta + post-dictatorship court bans (junta-era titles dated 1973). Cuba = Quinquenio-Gris-era suppressions (restricted where withheld rather than formally decreed). Held: Cuba 'La fiesta vigilada' (author-level), Chile Cortázar 'Reunión' (short story, not a book). Deduped against existing BR/CL/CU records.

entries.title: A Volúpia do Pecado
entries.description_ban: One of 18 Cassandra Rios novels banned by the DCDP during the military dictatorship; her debut, centred on a love story between two women, vetoed under 'morals and good customs'. Exact year per Reimão's documented list (proxy 1970).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; documented in Reimão's SciELO study.

entries.title: Nicoleta Ninfeta
entries.description_ban: Erotic novel among the 18 Cassandra Rios titles banned by the DCDP during the dictatorship. Exact year per Reimão's list (proxy 1970).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: Copacabana Posto Seis
entries.description_ban: Erotic novel among the Cassandra Rios titles banned by the DCDP. Exact year per Reimão's list (proxy 1970).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: A Paranóia
entries.description_ban: Listed among the 18 Cassandra Rios titles seized and banned by the DCDP. Exact year per Reimão's list (proxy 1970).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: Carniça
entries.description_ban: One of Adelaide Carraro's 13 DCDP-banned novels (Brazil's second most-banned author), mixing sex and social critique, judged against 'morality and good customs'. Exact year per Reimão's list (proxy 1970).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: Os Padres Também Amam
entries.description_ban: Adelaide Carraro novel on clergy sexuality banned by the DCDP. Exact year per Reimão's list (proxy 1970).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: A Falência das Elites
entries.description_ban: Politically themed Adelaide Carraro novel on her 13-title DCDP-banned list. Exact year per Reimão's list (proxy 1970).
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: A Revolução Brasileira
entries.description_ban: Marxist historical-political analysis banned by the DCDP; one of two Caio Prado titles on Reimão's documented censorship list.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DCDP ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: O Mundo do Socialismo
entries.description_ban: Caio Prado's pro-socialist political work, vetoed by the military regime.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: Aracelli, Meu Amor
entries.description_ban: Novel-reportage on the 1973 rape and murder of 8-year-old Araceli Crespo, naming the accused; censored at the request of the defendants' lawyers, and the author received death threats and went into hiding.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; Reimão (SciELO) and Wikipedia.

entries.title: Torturas e Torturados
entries.description_ban: Early exposé of torture under the military regime; on Reimão's documented banned non-fiction list.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: Memórias: A Verdade de um Revolucionário
entries.description_ban: Memoir of the general who launched the 1964 coup; banned despite (or because of) its insider account.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; Reimão (SciELO).

entries.title: Mal de amor
entries.description_ban: Poetry collection — the only book prohibited by the Pinochet regime after being printed and distributed; an erotic poem was deemed disrespectful to the Virgin Mary, and the editor was barred from distributing it.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific junta ban; documented by Memoria Chilena.

entries.title: Detenidos-desaparecidos: una herida abierta
entries.description_ban: One of the first works documenting the disappeared; DINACOS denied circulation permission in 1980 as 'conflictive', and it circulated clandestinely.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific DINACOS ban; documented by Libros Prohibidos CL.

entries.title: Impunidad diplomática
entries.description_ban: Court-ordered ban on entry and distribution (Santiago Appeals Court, May 1993; Supreme Court rejected the appeal in June 1993); the IACHR later recommended Chile lift the censorship.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific court ban; documented by Columbia Global Freedom of Expression and Wikipedia.

entries.title: El libro negro de la justicia chilena
entries.description_ban: Seized one day after launch via a State Security Law action by a Supreme Court minister; the author was forced into exile.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific seizure; documented by Memoria Chilena.

entries.title: El chilote Otey
entries.description_ban: Banned after the 1973 coup; the author was a Communist Party member and the work had been published by the Allende-era state press.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Junta-era ban; documented by BioBioChile (medium confidence).

entries.title: Buenas noches los pastores
entries.description_ban: First edition destroyed after the 1973 coup; the author was a prominent leftist singer-songwriter and Allende supporter.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Junta-era ban; documented by BioBioChile (medium confidence).

entries.title: Interpretación marxista de la historia de Chile
entries.description_ban: Marxist historiography of Chile; catalogued among titles destroyed and prohibited 1973–89.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Junta-era ban; catalogued by Libros Prohibidos CL (medium confidence).

entries.title: El capital, conceptos fundamentales
entries.description_ban: Marxist primer by a prime regime target; catalogued among titles destroyed and prohibited 1973–89.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Junta-era ban; catalogued by Libros Prohibidos CL (medium confidence).

entries.title: Teresa Batista cansada de guerra
entries.description_ban: Novel by the Brazilian Communist writer; catalogued among titles destroyed and prohibited under the Chilean dictatorship.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Junta-era ban; catalogued by Libros Prohibidos CL (medium confidence).

entries.title: Citas del presidente Mao Tse-Tung sobre la guerra popular
entries.description_ban: Compilation of Mao quotations; catalogued among titles destroyed and prohibited 1973–89.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Junta-era ban; catalogued by Libros Prohibidos CL (medium confidence).

entries.title: Texto Oficial de Ciencias Sociales, Enseñanza Básica
entries.description_ban: Allende-era official school social-studies textbook; catalogued among titles destroyed under the dictatorship.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Junta-era ban of a named textbook; catalogued by Libros Prohibidos CL (medium confidence).

entries.title: Los siete contra Tebas
entries.description_ban: Won the 1968 UNEAC theatre prize but was published with a declaration condemning its 'ideological deviations'; never sold in Cuban bookstores, removed from libraries, and Arrufat was ostracised for some 14 years.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented by in-cubadora and Cubadebate.

entries.title: Dos viejos pánicos
entries.description_ban: Won the 1968 Casa de las Américas prize but, with Piñera's post-1971 ostracism, went unstaged in Cuba until the 1990s and his works unpublished/unreprinted on the island for years.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented via Wikipedia and Rialta (medium confidence).

entries.title: Hombres sin mujer
entries.description_ban: Pioneering homoerotic Cuban prison novel published in Mexico in 1938 because of censorship in Cuba, and suppressed afterward under prevailing 'morality'.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented in the Bulletin of Hispanic Studies (Liverpool UP, medium confidence).

entries.title: Termina el desfile
entries.description_ban: Short-story collection (stories written 1964–68); a success abroad but never published in Cuba and prohibited under the Castro regime.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific ban; documented via Wikipedia (medium confidence).

entries.title: Sur: latitud 13
entries.description_ban: Declared the 1992 Casa de las Américas winner, then the prize was reversed under State Security pressure on the jury; the book was withheld from publication until 1998.
entries.inclusion_rationale: Title-specific suppression; documented by Árbol Invertido and Cubanet (medium confidence).
